title: Community and Engagement Survey Privacy Notice
body: "Privacy notice  This Privacy Notice explains who is processing your data, the personal data we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and what your legal rights are.  The Department for Culture, Media and Sport (DCMS) and Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) have commissioned Verian Group UK Ltd (“Verian”) to carry out the Community and Engagement Survey.  The survey asks questions on a wide range of topics relating to your engagement with your community, and in the arts and cultural sectors, major events and live sport (more details on survey content are available in the About the survey page). We also collect demographic data, please see the “Sensitive data collection” section for more details.  The main responsibility for the survey will lie with DCMS, as specified in this privacy notice.  Please see the Who will Verian share your data with? section for more detail on any potential data sharing.  Who is the data controller?  The Department for Culture, Media, and Sport (DCMS) is the data controller of personal data provided by participants to the Community and Engagement Survey. The DCMS postal address is:  Department for Culture, Media and Sport 100 Parliament Street Westminster London SW1A 2BQ  You can contact the DCMS Data Protection Officer by email at: dpo@dcms.gov.uk  Who is the data processor?  DCMS uses a third-party data processor, Verian. Verian administers the Community and Engagement Survey on DCMS’s behalf and carries out statistical analyses of the survey results in accordance with our instructions. DCMS has a contract in place with Verian. This means that they cannot do anything with your personal data unless DCMS has instructed them to do it. Verian will not share your personal data with any organisation apart from DCMS, except where authorised. Verian’s postal address is:  Verian 4 Millbank Westminster London SW1P 3JA  Email address for queries about the Community and Engagement Survey: cesurvey@veriangroup.com  What is the legal basis for collecting and processing data?  DCMS is the government department responsible for delivering government policy on culture, media, sport, tourism and civil society. DCMS’s activities are based on the exercise of executive and statutory powers.  DCMS has an obligation to compile and publish Official Statistics relating to the policy areas they cover. Publishing Official Statistics derived from the Community and Engagement Survey is intended to:   inform the public about social and economic matters surrounding DCMS sectors assist in the development and evaluation of public policy, to refine and provide better services to the public regulate quality and inform changes to improve support for and development of DCMS sectors   DCMS’s legal basis for processing personal data on the Community and Engagement Survey is that it is necessary for the performance of a task in the public interest, as the information gathered helps DCMS to monitor the policies it oversees.  When Verian conducts surveys, their invitations/platforms and questionnaires clearly identify them and explain the purpose(s) of their contact and if applicable, the purpose for collecting your personal data.
